mnemonics: Eve Can't Decrypt Alice & Bob's Love Letters (ECDABLL): RSA Diffie-Hellman creates a shared secret key securely over an insecure channel, like sending a locked box to establish a private conversation.
keyPoints: RSA is used for authentication and secure key exchange, ensuring the parties are who they say they are.
The Diffie-Hellman protocol allows two parties to jointly establish a shared secret over an insecure channel.
The security relies on the computational difficulty of the Discrete Logarithm Problem (for D-H) and the Integer Factorization Problem (for RSA).
commonMistakes: Confusing RSA (used for authentication and key encapsulation in this context) with the Diffie-Hellman key agreement protocol; they are distinct algorithms combined for a secure exchange.
Assuming the protocol is vulnerable to a Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attack if not properly authenticated with RSA or another method.
